reasoning: The control SI-2-c focuses on ensuring that security-relevant software and firmware are up-to-date, which can be mapped to Kubernetes in several ways. Ensuring nodes are automatically upgraded and patched aligns with maintaining up-to-date software. Image vulnerability scanning ensures that container images do not have known vulnerabilities. Admission controllers can enforce policies that require security patches to be applied before deployment. Keeping the Kubernetes cluster version up-to-date ensures that all components benefit from the latest security patches.
